message.content:
### 1) Screen Purpose  
The screen is part of a **ride-hailing driver app (Didi Conductor)** and serves to **confirm the payment amount for a completed ride** (specifically a UberX service, per the button text). It displays the total fare the passenger ("Juan") will pay, including any applicable promotions, and prompts the driver to finalize the transaction.


### 2) UI Layout Patterns  
The layout follows a **vertical, single-column structure** with three distinct sections:  
- **Top Bar**: A header with a close button and title.  
- **Content Area**: The main section displaying payment details.  
- **Bottom Bar**: A prominent action button to complete the ride.  


### 3) Color Scheme  
- **Primary Color**: Red (#E61717 or similar) – used for the bottom action button ("COMPLETAR UBERX") to draw attention.  
- **Secondary Color**: Dark gray (#333333 or similar) – used for the title ("Cobrar el pago") and passenger name ("Juan pagará").  
- **Accent Color**: Blue (#007AFF or similar) – used for the "Promoción" label (to highlight a discount/promotion).  
- **Background Color**: White (#FFFFFF) – clean, neutral background for readability.  


### 4) Key Components and Their Placement  
#### Top Bar  
- **Close Button**: A black "X" icon (left-aligned) in the top-left corner, allowing the driver to exit the payment confirmation screen.  
- **Title**: "Cobrar el pago" (centered) in bold, dark gray text, indicating the screen’s purpose.  

#### Content Area  
- **Passenger Name**: "Juan pagará" (top of the content area) in medium-dark gray, smaller font than the title, identifying the paying passenger.  
- **Total Fare**: "ARS1,690" (center) in **extra-bold, large font** (e.g., 32px) – the most prominent element, showing the final amount.  
- **Promotion Label**: "Promoción" (below the fare) in blue, italicized text with a small blue diamond icon (left of the text) – indicates a discount or special offer applied to the fare.  
- **Right Arrow Icon**: A gray arrow (right-aligned next to the fare) – suggests additional details (e.g., fare breakdown) are available if tapped.  

#### Bottom Bar  
- **Action Button**: "COMPLETAR UBERX" (full-width, bottom-aligned) in white text on a red background, with a white right arrow icon (left of the text) – the primary call-to-action to finalize the ride and receive payment.  


### 5) User Flow State  
The user (driver) is in the **final step of a ride transaction**. They have completed the ride, and the app is displaying the total fare the passenger will pay (including promotions). The driver is expected to:  
- Review the fare and promotion details.  
- Tap the "COMPLETAR UBERX" button to confirm the payment and mark the ride as complete (triggering the passenger’s payment and the driver’s earnings).  

The screen is **static** (no loading indicators) and focused on a single action: finalizing the ride. The clear, high-contrast design ensures the driver can quickly verify the amount and proceed without confusion.
